preFix.title: Dishwasher vs handwashing: the water and energy comparison
preFix.subtitle: What the numbers say about which method uses less water and energy for a household of four
preFix.excerpt: A fully loaded dishwasher uses 9 to 11 litres per cycle. Handwashing a full day's dishes for four people uses 50 to 80 litres. The dishwasher wins on water use in every comparison that has been done, but only when the machine is fully loaded and running on the eco programme. This is the evidence-based comparison and the conditions that determine which method saves most.
preFix.sourceNotes: University of Bonn study (Rainer Stamminger, 2012) on dishwasher vs handwashing water consumption. WRAP 'Water in the home' evidence review. Energy Saving Trust dishwasher water labelling guidance. European Ecodesign Regulation (EU) 2019/2022 on household dishwashers.

content.text: fully loaded dishwasher on the eco programme
is lower than handwashing the same load. The advantage disappears when the machine runs half-loaded, on a non-eco cycle, or when handwashing uses a bowl with no running water.
Dishwasher, eco cycle, full load: 9 to 11 litres
Dishwasher, standard cycle, full load: 12 to 15 litres
Handwashing in a bowl with no running water, 2 bowls of water: 12 to 16 litres
Handwashing under running tap (common practice): 50 to 80 litres

The dishwasher uses 0.6 to 0.9 kWh per eco cycle. Heating 50 litres of water for handwashing in an electric hot water cylinder uses around 2.9 kWh. The dishwasher wins on energy too, but only against the hot-water method. Handwashing in cold water (rarely practiced) uses almost no energy.

The dishwasher wins when it is full. A half-loaded machine running daily uses the same water per cycle as a full machine, so the per-item use doubles. If you are running a dishwasher half-loaded, either wait until it is full or switch the small loads to careful bowl-handwashing.

Handwashing wins when there are only two or three items to wash: the setup and rinse cost of a dishwasher cycle makes it uneconomical for small loads. Washing one mug under a running tap for 20 seconds uses less than 0.5 litres; a full dishwasher cycle for one mug uses 9 to 11 litres.

EU Ecodesign labelling requires manufacturers to state rated water consumption per cycle from A (most efficient) to G. The rated water use for an A-rated 12-place-setting machine must be below 7.5 litres per cycle under the 2021 standard. Older A+++ machines tested under the previous standard may actually use more water than a new A-rated machine because the rating methodology changed. Look for the stated eco-cycle litre figure, not just the energy rating letter.
